asset_provenance.collision_strategy: visual avatar only; gameplay movement and collision use the procedural rover proxy
asset_provenance.known_limitations: Mesh generated from mesh-only TRELLIS output with Blender-authored PBR-style material slots; no external texture maps, no rig/animation, not Unity-imported.
asset_provenance.tool: OpenAI image reference, TRELLIS-image-large mesh-only export, Blender 5.1.1 cleanup/material/proxy export, Three.js GLTFLoader runtime import

asset_provenance.collision_strategy: visual landmark only; no generated mesh collision
asset_provenance.known_limitations: Mesh-only, not textured/PBR, not Blender-cleaned, not Unity-imported, not the hero rover asset.
asset_provenance.tool: TRELLIS-image-large mesh-only export; Three.js GLTFLoader runtime import

asset_provenance.collision_strategy: simple proxy box controlled by game state
asset_provenance.known_limitations: Not a generated GLB hero asset; Unity and Blender import are not claimed.

asset_provenance.collision_strategy: deterministic QA state transitions for gate validation
asset_provenance.known_limitations: No external mesh, texture, or material generation was used.
asset_provenance.tool: Three.js boxes, cylinder, and torus primitives

known_limitations: P1 now imports RW-ROVER-001 as an AI-assisted visual avatar with Blender-authored rover-v1 PBR-style material slots, and RW-CRATE-AI-001 as a visual landmark.
Imported AI-assisted GLBs are not used as gameplay collision; generated-mesh collision remains unproven and intentionally excluded.
RW-ROVER-001 has procedural material slots but no external texture maps, no rig/animation, and is not Unity-imported.
The deterministic QA path uses the prototype's local __prototypeGate API to avoid brittle pointer-lock automation.
